title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs go 1.26 and aws sdk for go v2 client contract from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.
Load immutable shared configuration once, construct scoped clients, pass context, and assign transport and credential lifetimes.
Use SDK pointers, enums, document types, output metadata, paginators, and nil handling without losing API semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Sdk Error Interface
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 5127 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect smithy operation, API, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving request evidence.

Inspect the Sdk Error Interface trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Sdk Error Interface.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect smithy operation, api, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.is and errors.as while preserving request evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Sdk Error Interface, keep Sdk Error Interface connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the review Runtime Sdk Version case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the review Runtime Sdk Version case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the review Runtime Sdk Version case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the review Runtime Sdk Version case

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Sdk Error Interface
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Sdk Error Interface: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 7460 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect smithy operation, API, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving request evidence.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Sdk Error Interface.
steps.why: Retrieving Sdk Error Interface through the concrete pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Sdk Error Interface, keep Sdk Error Interface connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect smithy operation, API, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving request evidence.
